stages.name: setup
stages.message: pyshacl 0.31.0, rdflib, datasets, anthropic installed in tasks/.venv-tasks (Python 3.12). Local copies of croissant-tasks-shapes.ttl and croissant-tasks.ttl used for validation.

stages.name: paper_analysis
stages.message: Paper read in full. Task definition, three domains (poetry, numerical, github_prs), micro-F1 + element-level exact match metric, and Table 3 baselines extracted. Two of 16 reported baselines materialized as TaskSolutions (claude-3-7-sonnet ±thinking).

stages.name: problem_generated
stages.message: absencebench_problem.jsonld written with three subTasks, shared OutputSpec referencing a RecordSet with a single repeated string Field, and EvaluationSpec declaring F1-Score and Exact Match.

stages.name: solutions_generated
stages.message: Two paper-reported TaskSolution files written (claude-3-7-sonnet, claude-3-7-sonnet-thinking). One ct2code-produced TaskSolution also lives in this directory (absencebench_claude-4-sonnet_solution.jsonld) - that one is documented in raw_outputs/<model>/manifest_*.json and is not part of this pdf2ct validation report.

stages.name: json_validity
stages.message: All four JSON-LD files parse as JSON and as RDF graphs (rdflib json-ld parser).

stages.name: shacl_conformance
stages.message: SHACL validation could not be performed. tasks/validator.py raises 'exists but is not a well-formed SHACL PropertyShape' on every input including tasks/testdata/valid_problem.jsonld. Root cause: TaskProblemShape in tasks/croissant-tasks-shapes.ttl uses an outer sh:property whose body is sh:or of alternatives, with no sh:path on the outer property shape. Strict pyshacl 0.22 onwards refuses this. Reproduced across pyshacl 0.22-0.31. Until the shapes are fixed, this run uses _structural_check.py (rdflib-only) as a substitute - see structural_check_results below.

upstream_issues.summary: Validator rejects every input due to a malformed PropertyShape in TaskProblemShape (sh:property with sh:or of alternative paths but no outer sh:path).
upstream_issues.suggested_fix: Lift the sh:or out of the inner sh:property and apply it as sh:or at the NodeShape level, with each alternative being a full sh:property with its own sh:path and sh:class. Reproduces on pyshacl 0.22-0.31.
upstream_issues.workaround: tasks/benchmark_examples/absencebench/runs/<run_id>/infra/_structural_check.py validates JSON parse + RDF parse + expected types/properties + spec leakage rules using rdflib only. Drop when shapes file is fixed.
